seo.title: 100 Embeddings & Vector Search resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical Embeddings & Vector Search resources for engineers.

content.intro: This resource provides a technical map for implementing embeddings and vector search, focusing on model selection, indexing strategies, and retrieval optimization. It targets developers moving beyond basic prototypes into production-grade semantic search and recommendation systems.

items.title: OpenAI text-embedding-3-small
items.description: Recommended starting point for general-purpose embeddings. Offers a 1536-dimension vector with a 'dimensions' parameter to reduce size without retraining.

items.title: Voyage AI voyage-2
items.description: High-performance model optimized for retrieval tasks. Consistently ranks at the top of the MTEB leaderboard for document search.

items.title: Cohere Embed v3
items.description: Features a 'compression' parameter for binary or int8 quantization, significantly reducing storage costs in vector databases.

items.title: Hugging Face sentence-transformers
items.description: Standard Python library for running open-source models like BGE-large or All-MiniLM-L6-v2 locally or on private infrastructure.

items.title: Jina Embeddings v2
items.description: Supports an 8k token context window, making it suitable for long-form document embedding where chunking might lose context.

items.title: MTEB Benchmark
items.description: Massive Text Embedding Benchmark. Use this to compare model performance across specific tasks like clustering, retrieval, and summarization.

items.title: Local Inference with ONNX Runtime
items.description: Convert Hugging Face models to ONNX format to run embeddings in Node.js or C# environments with minimal latency.

items.title: Mixedbread.ai bge-m3
items.description: A versatile model supporting multi-lingual, multi-functional (dense/sparse), and multi-granularity retrieval within a single embedding.

items.title: Infinity Embedding Server
items.description: A high-throughput, MIT-licensed inference server for deploying open-source embedding models via a REST API.

items.title: Google Vertex AI Embeddings
items.description: Enterprise-grade embedding models integrated with GCP, offering high rate limits and managed infrastructure.

items.title: pgvector for PostgreSQL
items.description: Extension enabling vector similarity search in Postgres. Best for teams already using RDS or Supabase to avoid adding new infra.

items.title: Qdrant
items.description: Rust-based vector database. Offers advanced payload filtering and high-performance HNSW indexing for production scale.

items.title: Pinecone Serverless
items.description: Managed vector database that scales based on usage. Ideal for applications with unpredictable traffic and large datasets.

items.title: Weaviate
items.description: Open-source vector DB with a GraphQL interface and native support for multi-modal data (images, video, text).

items.title: ChromaDB
items.description: Lightweight, developer-centric database often used for local RAG development and small-scale deployments.

items.title: Milvus
items.description: Cloud-native vector database designed for billion-scale vector search with decoupled storage and compute.

items.title: LanceDB
items.description: Serverless, disk-based vector database that stores data in the Lance format, optimized for random access and large-scale AI data.

items.title: FAISS (Facebook AI Similarity Search)
items.description: Library for efficient similarity search. Essential for building custom indexing pipelines or running in-memory searches.

items.title: Elasticsearch Vector Search
items.description: Utilizes k-NN plugin for vector search. Best for organizations already invested in the ELK stack for logging and search.

items.title: RedisVL
items.description: The Vector Library for Redis, enabling low-latency vector indexing and search directly within a Redis instance.

items.title: Hybrid Search (BM25 + Vector)
items.description: Combines traditional keyword search with semantic vector search using Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) for better accuracy.

items.title: Cohere Rerank
items.description: A cross-encoder model used as a second stage to re-score the top results from a vector search for higher precision.

items.title: HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World)
items.description: The industry-standard algorithm for approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) search, balancing speed and recall.

items.title: Product Quantization (PQ)
items.description: A compression technique that divides vectors into sub-vectors to reduce memory usage by up to 90% at a slight cost to accuracy.

items.title: Maximal Marginal Relevance (MMR)
items.description: A retrieval strategy that re-ranks results to reduce redundancy and increase the diversity of the returned items.

items.title: Parent-Document Retrieval
items.description: Technique where you embed small chunks for search but return the full parent document to the LLM for better context.

items.title: Query Expansion via LLM
items.description: Using an LLM to generate multiple versions of a user's query to catch more relevant vectors during retrieval.

items.title: Metadata Pre-filtering
items.description: Applying hard filters (e.g., date > 2023) before performing the vector similarity search to narrow the search space.

items.title: Contextual Compression
items.description: Filtering out irrelevant parts of retrieved documents before passing them to an LLM to save on token costs.

items.title: Multi-Vector Indexing
items.description: Assigning multiple vectors to a single document (e.g., summary vector and full text vector) to improve retrieval hits.

pro_tips: Always normalize your vectors to unit length (L2 norm) if your chosen similarity metric is Cosine Similarity, as many libraries perform faster with Dot Product on normalized data.
Do not change embedding models mid-project without a plan to re-index; a vector from OpenAI is mathematically incompatible with one from Cohere or Voyage.
Use 'Scalar Quantization' (int8) in your vector database to reduce memory footprint by 4x with less than 1% loss in retrieval accuracy.
Implement a 'Dead Letter Queue' for your embedding pipeline to handle documents that fail to embed due to token limits or API timeouts.
Store the source text or a unique ID in the vector database metadata to avoid a secondary database lookup during the retrieval phase.
